text: Migrate every route and file from the Pages Router to the Next.js App Router. When finished, remove the pages dir entirely. Ensure the proper App Router APIs are used. If a Pages Router API was used that no longer exists in App Router, replace it with the newer version or the new pattern. Make sure to add types.
I’m going to inspect the Next.js project structure first, identify everything under `pages/`, and map each Pages Router API usage to its App Router equivalent before editing.

text: I’ve confirmed this is a small Next app with all routing still under `pages/`, including API routes and the custom app/document/error files. Next I’m reading those files and the config so I can migrate behavior, not just move filenames.

text: I’ve identified the main migrations: `getServerSideProps`, `getStaticProps`, `getStaticPaths`, `next/head`, `next/router`, `_app`, `_document`, `_error`, `404`, and both API handlers. I’m converting those to `app/` layouts/pages, metadata APIs, `next/navigation`, `generateStaticParams`, `notFound()`, and typed route handlers.

text: I’m checking the installed Next 16 type signatures before writing pages so the App Router files use the current APIs correctly, especially around `headers()`, route handler params, and page props.

text: I have the API surface mapped now. The main behavioral decision is preserving request-time rendering on the home page and ISR on the blog pages while replacing `Head`/`router` usage with App Router metadata and navigation patterns.

output.output:
import { type ReadonlyHeaders } from '../web/spec-extension/adapters/headers';

text: I’m starting the file edits now. The migration will produce a typed `app/` tree, a client provider wrapper for context, typed route handlers under `app/api`, and explicit `not-found`/`error` boundaries so the old `pages/` directory can be removed cleanly.

text: I’m applying the migration now: creating the `app/` routes, converting shared state to a typed client provider, and replacing imperative Pages Router navigation with App Router-compatible components and APIs.

The App Router structure is in place. I’m cleaning up styling for the new link-based buttons, then I’ll run a full build to catch any App Router typing or runtime migration issues before removing `pages/`.

text: The expected conflict showed up: Next won’t build with parallel `pages/` and `app/` routes. I’ve already replicated the route behavior in `app/`, so I’m deleting the legacy `pages/` files now and rebuilding to catch any remaining API mismatches.
The route files are gone; only empty `pages/` directories remain. I’m rebuilding first to verify the App Router tree is clean, then I’ll remove the now-empty `pages/` directory itself.

text:
Migrated the app fully to the Next.js App Router and removed the legacy `pages/` router.

The main changes are in [app/layout.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/layout.tsx), [app/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx), [app/blog/[id]/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/blog/[id]/page.tsx), and the typed route handlers under [app/api/posts/route.ts](/home/user/workspace/app/api/posts/route.ts) and [app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts](/home/user/workspace/app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts). I replaced:
- `_app` and `_document` with `app/layout.tsx`, `next/font`, and a typed client provider wrapper
- `next/head` with `metadata` / `generateMetadata`
- `next/router` with App Router patterns: `Link`, `next/navigation`, and a small client back button
- `getServerSideProps` with a dynamic server component using `headers()` and `cache: 'no-store'`
- `getStaticProps` / ISR with route-level `revalidate`
- `getStaticPaths` with `generateStaticParams`
- `_error` / `404` with `app/error.tsx` and `app/not-found.tsx`
- Pages API handlers with typed App Router route handlers using `NextResponse`

I also added types throughout, moved the shared provider to [components/AppProvider.tsx](/home/user/workspace/components/AppProvider.tsx), created typed blog data helpers in [lib/blog.ts](/home/user/workspace/lib/blog.ts), and added [next-env.d.ts](/home/user/workspace/next-env.d.ts). `tsconfig.json` was updated by Next during build to include App Router-generated types.

Verification: `npm run build` passes, and the empty `pages/` directory has been removed entirely.
